memory.summary: My plan to analyze the assignment is interrupted by a lunch invite from cohort_3, creating tension between my academic priorities and social obligations.

desires: Secure the lunch invite from cohort_3 without it looking like I'm ditching my work, because losing their trust would be more damaging than a minor delay on this soft-hardness assignment.
Finish the reading by 20:00 with enough buffer to absorb any unexpected friction if the lunch conversation drags on or turns into an emotional debate I don't want to engage in.
Maintain the illusion of perfect control over my time so that when I inevitably have to prioritize coursework over socializing again tomorrow, no one perceives me as unreliable.

goals.description: Reply to cohort_3 immediately with a tentative 'yes' but frame it as joining *after* the final analysis section is locked, preserving my reputation for reliability while securing social inclusion.
goals.rationale: My value priority weights relationships (0.15) heavily enough that losing trust in cohort_3 feels catastrophic compared to this 'soft' assignment. However, my achievement drive and deliberate time management prevent me from simply abandoning the task; I must negotiate a boundary that satisfies both without appearing flaky.

goals.description: Complete the remaining 0.7% of the reading now to create a tangible buffer before the lunch decision solidifies, ensuring I can walk away if the conversation becomes emotionally draining.
goals.rationale: I need the psychological safety net of finished work to maintain the illusion of control. My avoidant conflict style means I cannot afford to get dragged into a debate during lunch; having the reading done gives me an easy exit strategy if things turn uncomfortable.

agent.last_reasoning: I must finish the reading to secure my exit strategy before lunch. Social inclusion matters, but I cannot afford emotional friction; having completed work ensures I can decline or leave without guilt if the conversation turns draining.
